what_it_does: A second-pass critical review of an in-app guided-review surface, reporting hands-on driving of the populated UI, a FUNCTION/FORM scorecard, a gap list, and a verdict on whether the experience feels like a product or dev-stuff.
observations: The prior pass could not drive the populated surface; this pass succeeded and overturns several of its pessimistic calls.
The surface is objectively on the design-system/corpus tokens: 280 live CSS custom properties, Fraunces + IBM Plex Mono, gold-on-dark, and every chrome element carries a data-ui-ref.
The guided tour genuinely leads: spotlight drives to the element, shows 1 of 3 / 2 of 3 progress, and narrates in a corpus 3-leg, plain-language voice.
Click+talk works at altitude: with a mockup-focused locus the RHM replies grounded in the mockup HTML, and /api/chat/stream returns incremental NDJSON parts.
Two dev-stuff leaks remain: tldraw editor chrome bleeds through the review surface, and a responsive-overflow stylesheet renders its own source as visible toolbar text.
The make-or-break F2 comment-to-generate loop is broken: comments post to the annotation store, but generate reads the retired JSONL store, so the operator path cannot complete.
The scorecard uses provenance labels (by-use, code-confirmed, couldnt-observe) and separates FUNCTION from FORM for criteria A through O.
The gap list sorts findings into built-and-good, built-but-dev-stuff, not-built, and couldnt-observe.
Harness/stability notes are explicitly separated from product verdicts: bridge died twice, mobile resize did not take, POST /api/mockup-feedback did not persist.
Screenshots are listed as FORM evidence with file names and what each shows.
standouts: F2 comment-to-generate seam is the make-or-break break: the engine works but the operator loop does not.
tldraw editor chrome leaking into the review surface is the top FORM gap.
The guided tour genuinely leads and reads at-altitude, overturning the prior pass.
The surface is measurably on the design system, not deliberately unstyled dev-stuff.
conventions: Uses provenance labels (by-use, code-confirmed, couldnt-observe) to type every claim.
Scores each criterion across FUNCTION and FORM separately.
Avoids green-paint and false negatives: a thing not seen is couldnt-observe, not not-met.
Distinguishes product defects from harness/session artifacts in their own section.
Cites screenshots, code paths, and API endpoints as evidence.
Maps requirements to short criterion IDs (A1, F2, L1, etc.).
concerns: Several FUNCTION verdicts rely on code-confirmed rather than by-use, which the file itself notes is present but not run.
A large share of criteria are marked couldnt-observe, so the verdict leaves many lanes unproven rather than passed.
The strong overturn of the prior pass rests on one successful hands-on session with a temporary store and isolated bridge.
The architectural contradiction between the annotation store and the JSONL feedback store is named but not resolved.
notes: A no-green-paint, hands-on review that says the guided surface is mostly built and product-like, with two chrome leaks and one broken comment-to-generate seam blocking the founding promise.
questions: What is the root cause of the bridge process deaths?
Is the absence of mockup:// from SCHEMES an oversight or an intentional scope cut?
How will the comment store and generate-feedback store be unified?
What is the intended mobile verification path since the 390 resize did not take?
Who is Tim and what decisions are explicitly delegated to them?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a critical review of the guided-review surface by use; actual content matches, while also serving as a correction to the prior pass and a prioritization memo for the F2 seam and chrome leaks.
intention_signals: To deliver an honest, use-driven verdict on whether the guided-review surface fulfills its product promise, correct an earlier overly pessimistic review, and name the exact blockers that must be fixed next.
novelty: A deliberate by-use epistemology with provenance labels and a FUNCTION/FORM scorecard that separates harness artifacts from product verdicts, plus an explicit evidence-based overturn of a prior review.
contribution: A reusable, evidence-typed assessment template (by-use/code-confirmed/couldnt-observe), a FUNCTION/FORM scorecard, and a named taxonomy of built-good/built-but-leaky/not-built gaps that can serve as acceptance criteria.
summary_for_embedding: A second-pass critical review of a guided-review surface reporting that the tour and chat work, the surface is on the design system, but the comment-to-generate loop is broken because comment and generate use different stores, and tldraw chrome leaks into the review view.
intention_for_embedding: To give an honest hands-on verdict on the guided-review surface, correct a prior review, and identify the specific make-or-break blockers and dev-stuff leaks to fix next.
